mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 in the CPIAUCSL index triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved profit expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to correlate with 20d SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15) or 20d returns remain negative despite INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate above 9.5% signals weak labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment, or UNRATE fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate elevated consumer demand for durable goods, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST fails to correlate with 20d SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15) or 20d returns remain negative despite HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, creating positive momentum for S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and INDPRO growth fail to predict 60d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or returns remain negative despite concurrent expansion in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate monetary tightening to combat inflation, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict 20d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or returns remain positive despite tightening conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains above 130,712 thousand signal strong labor market demand, increasing consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll growth, or PAYEMS fails to correlate with 5d returns (Pearson r < 0.15).
